research.description: Research the topic '{topic}'. Your deliverable is a comprehensive research notes file at workspace/research_notes.md. Structure your notes under these sections: (1) Overview and Key Definitions, (2) Major Frameworks and Their Architecture, (3) Comparative Analysis and Trade-offs, (4) Production Patterns and Challenges, (5) Bibliography. Cite at least 8 sources using [AuthorYear] inline citation format. Minimum 1500 words total.
research.expected_output: A structured Markdown file at workspace/research_notes.md with 5 sections, minimum 1500 words, and at least 8 [AuthorYear] inline citations with full references in the Bibliography section.

write.description: Using the research notes at workspace/research_notes.md, write 6 Markdown chapters for an article on '{topic}'. Save each chapter to workspace/chapters/. Chapters: ch01_introduction.md (~400 words, introduce the topic), ch02_architectures.md (~500 words, agent architectures and topologies), ch03_frameworks.md (~500 words, LangChain vs CrewAI vs LangGraph comparison with a Markdown table), ch04_production.md (~400 words, production patterns including a mathematical relationship you mark with [NEEDS_FORMULA]), ch05_bidi.md (~400 words, write at least 3 full paragraphs in Hebrew mixed with English technical terms), ch06_casestudy.md (~300 words, case study of this pipeline). Each chapter must include at least 2 inline citations.
write.expected_output: 6 Markdown files in workspace/chapters/: ch01_introduction.md, ch02_architectures.md, ch03_frameworks.md (with comparison table), ch04_production.md (with [NEEDS_FORMULA] marker), ch05_bidi.md (with Hebrew paragraphs), ch06_casestudy.md.

edit.description: Review and polish all 6 chapters in workspace/chapters/. For each chapter: (1) enforce consistent terminology, (2) verify all [AuthorYear] citations are defined in the research notes bibliography, (3) ensure tables use pipe | syntax for clean LaTeX conversion, (4) verify ch05_bidi has at least 3 Hebrew paragraphs, (5) mark any mathematical relationship not yet marked as [NEEDS_FORMULA]. Save each polished chapter as ch0N_edited.md in workspace/chapters/.
edit.expected_output: 6 edited Markdown files in workspace/chapters/: ch01_edited.md through ch06_edited.md. Each improved for clarity, citation correctness, and LaTeX readiness.

latex.description: Convert the approved Markdown chapters in workspace/chapters/*_edited.md to a complete LaTeX article. Steps: (1) Read all 6 edited chapters. (2) Generate a bar chart using ChartGeneratorTool showing framework comparison scores — save as latex/figures/agent_topology.png. (3) Write latex/chapters/ch01_introduction.tex through ch06_casestudy.tex, converting Markdown to LaTeX. Include \resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{\input{figures/crew_architecture.tikz}} in ch01 (CRITICAL: use \resizebox wrapper or TikZ diagram will overflow page margins). In ch04, replace every [NEEDS_FORMULA] marker with \begin{equation}...\end{equation} — CRITICAL: fancy formula, not plain text. In ch05 (Hebrew chapter), follow these STRICT BiDi rules: (A) BEFORE \chapter{}, call \hebrewheadingformat to right-align RTL headings; wrap the chapter title in \texthebrew{}: \chapter{\texthebrew{כותרת}}. (B) BEFORE \chapter{}, inject the Hebrew TOC override — this sets page-number on LEFT, dots, title on RIGHT for TOC entries (exact block in latex_skill SKILL.md). (C) Hebrew section headings MUST use BOTH \texthebrew{} AND \mbox{\setLTR\enspace\thesection}: \section[\texthebrew{כותרת}]{\texthebrew{כותרת}\mbox{\setLTR\enspace\thesection}} — using only \texthebrew{} without \mbox{\setLTR} still reverses the section number. (D) Hebrew body paragraphs go in \begin{hebrew}...\end{hebrew}. (E) ALL English text inside Hebrew blocks MUST use \foreignlanguage{english}{term} — otherwise luabidi reverses characters. This applies to: package names (\foreignlanguage{english}{\texttt{polyglossia}}), abbreviations (\foreignlanguage{english}{BiDi}), brand names, and any Latin text. English inside \texthebrew{} headings also needs \foreignlanguage{english}{...} wrapping. (F) AFTER the Hebrew sections (before English section 5.4), restore LTR: \addtocontents{toc}{\protect\etocstandardlines} then \defaultheadingformat. English sections in the same chapter use normal \section{title} format. (4) Write latex/bib/references.bib with all citations in BibTeX format. (5) Run: lualatex main.tex, then biber main, then lualatex main.tex, then lualatex main.tex (4 passes total). Verify the .log has no unresolved references. CRITICAL: write ONLY valid LaTeX to .tex files — never append debug text, markdown fences, or plain-text explanations after the LaTeX content.
latex.expected_output: latex/output/uoh-sqak-article.pdf — a PDF of at least 15 pages with cover sheet, table of contents, 6 chapters, bibliography. The PDF must contain: at least one fancy equation (not plain text), at least one figure, the TikZ crew architecture diagram with \resizebox wrapper, at least one table, the BiDi Hebrew chapter with correct LTR section numbers in TOC, and clickable bibliography links.
